title: scurrula atropurpurea
intro: Scurrula atropurpurea, also known as the Indian mistletoe or poisonwood, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host tree, usually in tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as having antiviral and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Scurrula atropurpurea can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
